paper.title: The Vibration Analysis Method of the Thin Chamber Equipment by Node Matching
paper.abstract: This study introduces an innovative and robust approach for the vibration analysis of thin chamber equipment, specifically applied to electrical switchboards using a node matching technique. To ensure structural stability under seismic excitation, the research proposes the integration of dampers and pivots as new anti-vibration devices (AVD). These devices are designed to mitigate vibrations caused by earthquakes and inertia forces resulting from the equipment’'s mass. The core of the analysis involves a high-fidelity finite element (FE) model developed in Solidworks. The methodology employs a node matching approach to link complex components, such as the top cover casting parts and the AVDs. Each structural component was meshed using 8-node hexahedron elements, totaling 377,199 elements and 712,662 nodes, with a maximum mesh size of 30 mm to ensure the highest level of simulation accuracy. Experimental validation was conducted via shaker table tests to verify the dynamic characteristics. The results demonstrate a resonance frequency of approximately 6 Hz, with the AVDs reducing maximum relative displacement by at least 50%, proving the effectiveness of the node matching analysis in predicting and controlling vibrations for thin-walled industrial equipment. | ry 2 a - tone tg ~ 더 대 1: er 4 = 5 ! NE tet 82 19 Lh. . ‘ 기 10-7 10” 101 10? 로 — = Frequency (Hz)
